name: Eddie Pepitone
bio: Eddie Pepitone is an American stand-up comedian, character actor, and podcast host known for a dark, alternative style of ranting, self-deprecating, politically charged comedy that earned him the nickname the Bitter Buddha. A staple of the Los Angeles scene, he gained wider notice through early appearances on the WTF with Marc Maron podcast and recurring sketch turns as a heckler on Conan O'Brien's late-night shows. His work is documented in the 2012 film The Bitter Buddha.

timeline.ev: Releases the sketch album The Big Push
Releases first stand-up album A Great Stillness
The documentary The Bitter Buddha released to positive reviews
Releases the Netflix special In Ruins
Releases For the Masses, named the funniest special of the year by The New York Times

accolades: For the Masses named the funniest comedy special of 2020 by The New York Times
Subject of the acclaimed 2012 documentary The Bitter Buddha
Won an episode of Comedy Central's @midnight (2014)
